report_name: LockBit and ShinyHunters Claim Major Breaches at Citizens Bank, Canada Life, and Law Firm

threat_actors.name: LockBit
threat_actors.description: A ransomware gang that allegedly exfiltrated and posted data from Bardehle Pagenberg. The group is known for double extortion tactics, involving data encryption and exfiltration, and using tools like Cobalt Strike for lateral movement.
threat_actors.goals: Financial extortion through ransomware and data leakage.

threat_actors.name: ShinyHunters
threat_actors.description: A data broker group that claimed a breach at Canada Life. The group is known for large-scale data breaches, data theft, and selling stolen databases on dark web markets. They typically exploit web application vulnerabilities to gain access.
threat_actors.goals: Data theft for sale on dark web markets.

threat_actors.name: Everest
threat_actors.description: A cybercriminal group that claimed an attack on Citizens Bank. Their tactics are similar to ShinyHunters, focusing on data exfiltration for extortion or sale.
threat_actors.goals: Data exfiltration for extortion or sale.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: LockBit allegedly encrypted files on the victim's (Bardehle Pagenberg) network as part of their double extortion ransomware attack.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: LockBit exfiltrated sensitive data to an external location before encrypting the victim's files to use as leverage for payment.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters is known to exploit vulnerabilities in web applications, such as SQL injection, to gain access to backend databases.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: ShinyHunters is adept at exploiting misconfigured cloud storage buckets to exfiltrate large databases.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: Ransomware groups like LockBit commonly delete volume shadow copies to prevent system recovery.

malware.name: LockBit
malware.description: The ransomware payload used by the LockBit group to encrypt victim files and conduct double extortion attacks.

tools.name: Cobalt Strike
tools.description: Used by the LockBit gang for lateral movement within victim networks after gaining initial access.

identities.name: Bardehle Pagenberg
identities.description: A major European patent law firm allegedly breached by the LockBit ransomware gang, leading to potential intellectual property theft.

identities.name: Canada Life
identities.description: A large insurance and financial services provider in Canada allegedly breached by the ShinyHunters data broker group.

identities.name: Citizens Bank
identities.description: A major U.S. retail bank that was the claimed victim of a data breach by the Everest group.

infrastructure.name: LockBit Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the LockBit group to publish data from victims who do not pay the ransom, as part of their double extortion strategy.

infrastructure.name: ShinyHunters Data Marketplace
infrastructure.description: A dark web marketplace where the ShinyHunters group sells large databases stolen from breached organizations.

infrastructure.name: Everest Leak Site
infrastructure.description: A dark web site used by the Everest group to leak data for extortion or sale.

indicators.name: Ransomware Behavior - VSS Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: Volume Shadow Copy Deletion via VssAdmin
id: 2b8b3b3e-5b7a-4b6e-8f3d-2e9b9c9a9b9c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware like LockBit to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breach/citizensbank-everest
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-20
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'delete shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the deletion of volume shadow copies using vssadmin, a common ransomware behavior to prevent recovery.

indicators.name: Cobalt Strike Default Beaconing Pattern
indicators.pattern:
title: Cobalt Strike Default Beaconing Pattern
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects default process names and command-line patterns associated with Cobalt Strike beacons, a tool used by groups like LockBit.
references:
  - https://www.breachsense.com/breach/citizensbank-everest
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-20
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - '-nop -w hidden'
      - 'downloadstring'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects process execution patterns typical of Cobalt Strike, a tool used by LockBit for lateral movement.
